mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when RSAFS shows consistent growth over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment that constrains economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL is above 3.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate healthy investment and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods identified as strong economic expansion, high housing starts do not correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side weakness, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a monthly decline in INDPRO greater than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer income and corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average during periods of sustained positive PAYEMS growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decrease in the Federal Funds Rate indicates aggressive monetary easing by the central bank, intended to stimulate the economy, which typically boosts asset prices and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 35% of instances following a 0.5 percentage point decline in FEDFUNDS over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but retail sales (RSAFS) are declining, it suggests a divergence where overall economic output is not translating into consumer spending, indicating potential future weakness for S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% YoY AND RSAFS growth < 0% YoY
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive on average when GDPC1 growth is above 2% YoY and RSAFS growth is negative YoY.
